page: UpCloud GPU-192xCPU-1920GB-8xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for high-density parallel computing. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, featuring 192 shared virtual CPUs and 1920.0 GB of system memory, which yields a ratio of 10.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance operates on the KVM hypervisor and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This server configuration integrates eight NVIDIA B200 GPUs from the Blackwell family, delivering a combined total of 1536 GB of video RAM. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, meaning storage resources must be provisioned and attached externally.

Given the inclusion of eight Blackwell-generation GPUs and a massive memory footprint, this instance represents a high-end hardware class optimized for resource-intensive workloads. It is designed for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning model training, massive LLM inference, and complex data science computations that require substantial parallel processing capabilities and high VRAM capacity.
description: UpCloud GPU-192xCPU-1920GB-8xB200 is a high-density GPU-accelerated server instance designed for massive parallel workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture and running on the KVM hypervisor, it features 192 shared vCPUs and 1920.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.0 GB of RAM per core. The hardware configuration is highlighted by eight NVIDIA B200 Blackwell GPUs, which deliver a total of 1536 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, requiring external storage attachments. Given its extreme resource density and advanced GPU architecture, this server is engineered for demanding workloads such as large-scale artificial intelligence training, large language model inference, and intensive data science computations.
